title: Structural thermal solutions for display devices
abstract: In one aspect of the present disclosure, a display system is disclosed, such as a wearable head-mounted display (HMD), that includes an internal system housing and a movable display stack. The display stack includes a display panel, a carrier supporting the display panel, and a heat sink that are secured together such that the display panel, the carrier, and the heat sink are movable in unison within the internal system housing to adjust focus of the display panel. The carrier and/or the heat sink respectively include first and second thermal solutions to facilitate heat transfer away from the display panel, which may include a material having high thermal-conductivity properties and/or any structure that is configured to increase the exposed surface area of the carrier and/or the heat sink, such as projections or extensions (e.g., fins), recesses, slits, openings, or the like.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A wearable head-mounted display (HMD), comprising: an internal system housing defining an interior space; and a display stack positioned within the interior space, the display stack including: a display panel; a carrier supporting the display panel, the carrier including a first thermal solution to facilitate heat transfer away from the display panel; and a heat sink including a second thermal solution to facilitate heat transfer away from the display panel, wherein the display panel, the carrier, and the heat sink are secured together such that the display panel, the carrier, and the heat sink are movable in unison within the internal system housing to adjust focus of the display panel. 2. The HMD of claim 1, wherein the display panel is positioned between the carrier and the heat sink. 3. The HMD of any of claims 1 to 2, further including an actuator in mechanical cooperation with the display stack to reposition the display stack within the internal system housing. 4. The HMD of claim 3, wherein the actuator is secured to the internal system housing. 5. The HMD of any of claims 3 to 4, wherein the actuator is mechanically connected to the carrier. 6. The HMD of any of claims 3 to 4, wherein the actuator is mechanically connected to the heat sink. 7. The HMD of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the display stack is mechanically connected to the internal system housing to support the display stack during movement within the internal system housing. 8. The HMD of any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the display stack and the internal system housing include corresponding guide members to facilitate movement of the display stack in relation to the internal system housing. 9. The HMD of claim 8, wherein the corresponding guide members included on the internal system housing and the display stack are configured for relative linear movement. 10. The HMD of claim 9, wherein the relative linear movement is such that the display stack is repositionable between a multitude of positions along a longitudinal axis of the HMD to adjust focus of the display panel and cause air circulation within the HMD to facilitate heat dissipation from the display panel. 11. The HMD of any of claims 1 to 10, wherein the first and second thermal solutions each include at least one surface discontinuity to increase exposed surface area of the carrier and the heat sink. 12. The HMD of claim 11, wherein the at least one surface discontinuity is configured as one or more extensions depending outwardly from exposed surfaces of the carrier and the heat sink and/or one or more recesses extending inwardly into the carrier and the heat sink. 13. The HMD of any of claims 11 to 12, wherein the first and second thermal solutions include one or more thermally-conductive materials. 14. A method of reducing heat within a wearable display system including a display panel, the method comprising: providing at least one thermal solution on a movable display stack supporting the display panel, the at least one thermal solution including one or more extensions depending from exposed surfaces of the display stack and/or one or more recesses extending inwardly into the display stack to increase exposed surface area and heat dissipation in the display stack; and repositioning the display stack within an internal system housing of the HMD to 

 intake and expel air from the internal system housing such that air flows across the at least one thermal solution to transfer heat away from the display panel. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the movable display stack includes: a display panel including opposing first and second sides; a chassis positioned proximate the first side of the display panel, the chassis including a metallic material and at least one surface discontinuity to increase exposed surface area and heat dissipation in the chassis; and a thermal conductor positioned proximate the second side of the display panel, the thermal conductor including at least one surface discontinuity to increase exposed surface area and heat dissipation in the thermal conductor.
